page: Amazon Web Services m5a.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The system includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile characterized by a tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is limited, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests scoring in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by middle 50% rankings in stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Notably, the hardware performs strongly in cryptography, with the Passmark CPU encryption test placing in the top 25%, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets.

Memory bandwidth and database operations, such as Redis SET and static web serving, consistently perform within the average range. For machine learning, the instance handles small-model LLM inference at average speeds, though text generation on medium models drops to the bottom 25%. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5a.12xlarge offers a balanced resource density for multi-threaded workloads that do not require high single-core throughput. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, multi-threaded web serving, database operations, and moderately demanding enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m5a.12xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 24 physical cores, 48 vCPUs, and 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps without local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks highlight a significant tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and text processing rank in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average range. Cryptographic execution is a notable strength, scoring in the top 25% due to dedicated hardware instructions. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads that can leverage the high core count without requiring peak single-core performance. It is suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, web serving, and database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5a.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors, offering balanced multi-core performance and strong cryptographic capabilities.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5a.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and AMD EPYC 7571 processors.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring forty-eight virtual cores and high-capacity memory optimized for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 48 vCPUs and 192 GB DDR4 RAM
Delivers strong cryptographic performance, ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
Features a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Presents a tradeoff with lower single-core performance but average multi-core scaling
Well-suited for multi-threaded web serving, database operations, and general-purpose enterprise workloads
